title: MAP 6.13: Implement liveness reaper (real code from TASK-150 spec)
description: Build the actual liveness/reaper code from map-liveness-reaper-spec.md: a script that derives per-agent liveness state (last_seen, active_task, lane, state, state_since, stale_after, evidence) from agents/status.json, hcom list/events, and map.db task claims; applies the timeout policy defaults; and implements the action ladder (suspect -> nudge -> reclaim -> dead-letter -> broken) writing canonical events for every action. Read map-liveness-reaper-spec.md, scripts/limit_watcher.py (existing partial implementation to extend, not replace), db/claims.py, and agents/status.json before editing.
acceptance_criteria: Script computes the 7 state-vocabulary values (alive/working/blocked/idle/suspect/broken/standby) correctly against fixture agent/task/hcom data.
Reaper actions (nudge/reclaim/dead-letter) only occur via sanctioned DB helpers (db/claims.py) and are followed by validate_task_mirrors.py passing.
Every reaper action appends a canonical event with the fields the spec requires.
Dead-letter records are replayable back to READY without hand-editing task_graph.json, per a documented replay command and a focused test.

Circuit-breaker signals (agent_stale_once, agent_reclaimed_task, agent_repeated_stale, agent_broken, system_liveness_degraded) start accounting-only, per the spec's explicit non-blocking-by-default rule.
